decision_point: In an adult with a diabetic foot abscess or wet gangrene with systemic sepsis, is urgent surgical debridement or drainage or medical (antibiotics-alone) management preferred?
options: Urgent surgical debridement or drainage plus antibiotics
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult with a diabetic foot abscess or wet gangrene with systemic sepsis, is urgent surgical debridement or drainage or medical (antibiotics-alone) management preferred?
Options:
  1. Urgent surgical debridement or drainage plus antibiotics
  2. Antibiotics alone
label: settled
label_rationale: Source control is limb- and life-saving; antibiotics alone do not clear a collection or necrotic tissue.
